post.summary: Join or create an Abriyah shared room
post.description:
Match the calling rider into an Abriyah shared-ride room for their zone, opening a new room if none is available.

**Features:**
- Detects the Abriyah-enabled zone from the pickup point (PostGIS containment) and requires the dropoff to be inside the same zone.
- Computes a per-rider fare as base + round(haversine_km * per_km), in integer IQD; coordinates are WGS84 lng/lat.
- Finds an open room of the requested type in the zone, or creates one with the zone's max-riders and max-wait-seconds expiry.
- Creates a matched Abriyah trip for the rider and links it to the new room member.
- Publishes room.opened/room.joined and trip.requested realtime events.

**Behavior:**
- Validates `room_type` is 'mixed' or 'women_only'.
- Women-only requests require a female rider (gender 'f') and a zone that allows women-only rooms.
- On the 201 the room may already be 'dispatched' if it filled while a captain was assigned; a full but captain-less room stays open.

**Restrictions:**
- Rider JWT required; the rider's gender is read from their user record.
- One active room per rider; joining again while already in a room is rejected.
- Returns 400 when the pickup is outside any Abriyah zone, the dropoff is outside the pickup zone, women-only is not allowed in the zone, or `room_type` is invalid.

201.description: Rider matched into a room; returns the room, the new member, and the matched trip

400.description: Pickup not in an Abriyah zone, dropoff outside the zone, women-only not allowed here, or invalid room_type

403.description: JWT role is not 'rider' (role-gated route), or a women-only room is requested by a non-female rider

409.description: Rider is already in an active room

delete.summary: Leave the rider's active Abriyah room
delete.description:
Remove the calling rider from their current Abriyah room and cancel their matched trip.

**Behavior:**
- Looks up the rider's active room and member; cancels the linked trip (system actor for matched, admin actor for accepted) with reason 'changed_mind'.
- Removes the member from the room and decrements the room's rider count.
- Publishes a trip.cancelled realtime event.
- Leaving after a captain has locked the room is allowed but logged as a warning.

**Restrictions:**
- Rider JWT required.
- Returns 400 when the rider is not in any active room.

200.description: Rider left the room and the trip was cancelled

400.description: Rider is not in any active room

get.summary: Get an Abriyah room with its members
get.description:
Retrieve a single Abriyah room and the list of riders currently seated in it.

**Returns:**
- The room (zone, type 'mixed'|'women_only', status 'open'|'locked'|'dispatched'|'expired', max/current rider count, assigned captain, expiry and timestamps).
- The room members, each with their per-rider fare (integer IQD), distance, and linked trip id. Member pickup/dropoff geometry is not included here.

**Use Cases:**
- The Customer App polling a room the rider has joined for fill progress and dispatch state.

**Restrictions:**
- Rider JWT required; no ownership check is performed (any authenticated rider may read any room by id).

200.description: The room and its current members

404.description: No room exists with this id

post.summary: Accept and dispatch an Abriyah room (captain)
post.description:
Assign the calling captain to an open Abriyah room, lock it, and immediately dispatch it to all seated riders.

**Behavior:**
- Verifies the captain is approved and not already on another active room.
- Requires the room to be in 'open' status; locks it to the captain (publishes room.locked).
- Dispatches the room: transitions it to 'dispatched' and advances every member trip from matched to accepted with this captain (optimistic-locked; concurrent transitions are skipped).
- Triggers a best-effort reports rollup and enqueues a push notification to each rider.
- Returns the resulting dispatched room.

**Use Cases:**
- A captain taking the next available Abriyah pool in their zone.

**Restrictions:**
- Captain JWT required; the captain must have status 'approved'.
- Women-only rooms require a female captain (gender 'f').
- Returns 400 if the room is not 'open', 403 if the captain is not approved or fails the women-only gate, 409 if the captain already has an active room.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Room ID (UUID) of the open room to accept

200.description: The room after it was locked and dispatched to the captain

400.description: Room is not in 'open' status

403.description: Captain not approved, or a non-female captain accepting a women-only room

404.description: No room exists with this id

409.description: Captain already has an active room assignment

get.summary: List a room's rider details (assigned captain)
get.description:
Retrieve the full per-rider detail for a room the calling captain is assigned to.

**Returns:**
- The `room_id` and a member list with each rider's name, contact phone, pickup/dropoff as WKT points (e.g. "POINT(lng lat)"), per-rider fare in integer IQD, distance, and join time.

**Use Cases:**
- The Captain App showing the pickup sequence and rider contacts for an in-progress Abriyah pool.

**Restrictions:**
- Captain JWT required; only the captain assigned to the room may read it.
- Returns 403 when the caller is not the assigned captain, 404 when the room id does not exist.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Room ID (UUID) the captain is assigned to

403.description: Caller is not the captain assigned to this room

404.description: No room exists with this id

post.summary: Validate Abriyah pickup and dropoff pins
post.description:
Check whether both the pickup and dropoff pins fall inside a single Abriyah-enabled zone, for live feedback as the rider drags the map.

**Features:**
- Resolves the zone containing both pins (PostGIS); if `zone_id` is supplied, only that zone is checked.
- Returns `valid`, the resolved `zone_id` (when valid), and a human-readable `message` for inline display in the Customer App.

**Behavior:**
- This endpoint never returns an HTTP error for out-of-zone pins; invalid pins yield 200 with `valid:false` and an explanatory message.

**Access:**
- Public: no authentication required. It reads no rider data and mutates nothing (a stateless PostGIS check), mirroring `/api/trips/estimate` so the Customer App can validate pins as the rider drags the map before login.

200.description: Validation result with valid flag, resolved zone_id, and message

400.description: Malformed request body

get.description:
Retrieve the Admin Daily Activation Log: one row per captain per Asia/Baghdad calendar day, with a paginated, filtered list and a fee-sum footer.

**Features:**
- Filter by `date_from` / `date_to` (inclusive calendar-day bounds, Asia/Baghdad), `status` (pending | paid | waived), and `captain_id`.
- Offset pagination via `page` (1-based, min 1) and `page_size` (default 50, clamped to 1..=200).
- Each row joins the captain's name and phone plus the waiving admin's name (when waived).
- The body footer carries `total_fee_iqd` (sum of fees in integer IQD across the filtered set), `total` (matching-row count), `page`, and `page_size`.

**Use Cases:**
- Daily reconciliation of captain activation fees by an operator or finance admin.
- Drilling into a single captain's activation history via the `captain_id` filter.

parameters.name: date_from
parameters.description:
Inclusive lower bound on the activation `date` (Asia/Baghdad calendar
day, YYYY-MM-DD). Null means no lower bound.

parameters.name: date_to
parameters.description:
Inclusive upper bound on the activation `date` (Asia/Baghdad calendar
day, YYYY-MM-DD). Null means no upper bound.

parameters.name: status
parameters.description:
Filter by activation state: "pending" | "paid" | "waived" | "failed".
Null returns all states.

parameters.name: captain_id
parameters.description: Filter to a single captain's activations. Null returns all captains.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: 1-based page number to return. Null defaults to the first page.

parameters.name: page_size
parameters.description: Number of rows per page. Null applies the server default page size.

200.description: Paginated activation log rows with a fee-sum footer

get.description:
Fetch a single captain daily-activation record by its ID for the admin detail view.

**Returns:**
- The full `CaptainDailyActivation`: captain ID, the Asia/Baghdad calendar `date`, `fee_amount_iqd` (integer IQD), and `status` (pending | paid | waived).
- `collected_at` is set when `status` = paid (the captain wallet charge succeeded and stamped the collection time) and is null otherwise; `waived_by` and `waived_reason` are populated only when `status` = waived; `charge_error` carries the failure message only when a charge failed.

**Use Cases:**
- Inspecting one captain's activation status before deciding to waive the fee.
- Resolving a row opened from the Daily Activation Log.

404.description: No activation record with this ID

post.description:
Waive a captain's daily activation fee. Restricted to the super-admin role and requires an audited reason.

**Behavior:**
1. Trims the `reason` and rejects it when shorter than 10 characters (400).
2. Loads the activation record by ID; returns 404 when it does not exist.
3. Guards the current status: only a `pending` record can be waived. A record that is already `waived` or `paid` is rejected by the service guard (409); a `failed` record (its charge was rejected) passes the service guard but then loses the optimistic lock on the `WHERE status = 'pending'` update, so it also returns 409. In short, any non-pending status yields 409.
4. Sets `status` = waived and records `waived_by` (the calling admin) and the trimmed `waived_reason`.
5. Publishes a `beep.captain.activation_waived` realtime/audit event on the captain's channel capturing the before/after status.
6. Returns the updated `CaptainDailyActivation`.

**Use Cases:**
- Forgiving an activation fee for a captain as a goodwill or dispute resolution by a super-admin.

**Restrictions:**
- Super-admin role only; other roles receive 403.
- `reason` must be at least 10 characters after trimming.
- Only a `pending` record can be waived; any non-pending status (already `waived`, already `paid`, or `failed`) returns 409. Not idempotent: re-waiving an already-waived record returns 409.

200.description: Fee waived; the updated activation record

400.description: Reason is shorter than 10 characters

404.description: No activation record with this ID

409.description: Record is not pending (already waived, already paid, or failed) and cannot be waived

post.description:
Approve up to 100 pending captains in a single admin action.

**Features:**
- Runs each captain id through the full approval engine (document gate + state machine + `beep.captain.approved` event).
- Per-row independent processing: one bad id never aborts the batch.
- Optional `note` field is accepted but currently informational only.

**Behavior:**
1. Reject the call (400) if the id list is empty or has more than 100 items.
2. For each captain id, attempt engine approval.
3. An id that is missing, non-pending, or document-incomplete is recorded in `errors`, not returned as a 4xx.
4. Respond 200 with `succeeded`, `failed`, and per-failed-row `errors`.

**Use Cases:**
- Clearing a backlog of vetted captain applications from the Admin Dashboard.

**Restrictions:**
- Admin-only (riders are rejected with 403).
- List must be non-empty and at most 100 ids.

400.description: Empty list or more than 100 ids

401.description: Missing, invalid, or revoked bearer token

403.description: Authenticated as a rider (admin role required)

post.summary: Export trips as a JSON array
post.description:
Export trip rows matching a filter, as a flat JSON array for download or reporting.

**Features:**
- Filterable by `city_id`, `zone_id`, `trip_type`, `status`, and a request-time window (`from_date` / `to_date`, RFC3339).
- All filter fields are optional; omitting them exports across all dimensions.
- `limit` is clamped to `[1, 10_000]` and defaults to 1000 when omitted.

**Behavior:**
- Each row carries trip id, type, status, rider id, optional captain/zone ids, integer-IQD fare, distance in km, and request/completion timestamps.
- `captain_id`, `zone_id`, and `completed_at` are null until assigned/completed.

**Use Cases:**
- Pulling an operational trip dataset for a city or zone over a date range.

**Restrictions:**
- Admin-only (riders are rejected with 403).
- At most 10,000 rows are returned regardless of a larger requested limit.

401.description: Missing, invalid, or revoked bearer token

403.description: Authenticated as a rider (admin role required)

post.description:
Archive up to 50 zones in a single admin action.

**Features:**
- Per-row independent processing: one skipped zone never aborts the batch.
- On a successful archive, publishes a best-effort `beep.zone.archived` event (failure never fails the row).

**Behavior:**
1. Reject the call (400) if the id list is empty or has more than 50 items.
2. For each zone id, apply guards in order: not found, already archived, or has active trips / open rooms.
3. Any guard hit records a row error (e.g. `not found`, `already archived`, `has active trips or rooms`) instead of a 4xx.
4. Respond 200 with `succeeded`, `failed`, and per-failed-row `errors`.

**Use Cases:**
- Decommissioning several inactive service zones at once.

**Restrictions:**
- Admin-only (riders are rejected with 403).
- List must be non-empty and at most 50 ids.
- A zone with active trips or open rooms is skipped, not force-archived.

400.description: Empty list or more than 50 ids

401.description: Missing, invalid, or revoked bearer token

403.description: Authenticated as a rider (admin role required)

get.description:
Retrieve every operating city, active and inactive alike.

**Features:**
- Returns all cities with no filtering or pagination
- Includes inactive cities (those not yet activated for multi-city expansion)
- Each row carries IANA timezone (e.g. Asia/Baghdad) and ISO currency (e.g. IQD)

**Use Cases:**
- Populate the city picker in the Admin Dashboard
- Audit which cities are live before activating zones or trips

post.description:
Register a new operating city.

**Features:**
- Timezone defaults to Asia/Baghdad when omitted
- Currency defaults to IQD when omitted
- Active flag defaults per the repository when omitted

**Behavior:**
- Inserts a new city row and returns the persisted record with its generated UUID

**Restrictions:**
- Admin role required
- name and name_ar are required

400.description: Malformed JSON body (syntax error)

422.description: JSON body is missing a required field (e.g. name, name_ar) or has a wrong-typed field

get.description:
Fetch a single city record by its UUID.

**Returns:**
- Full city record: name, Arabic name, IANA timezone, ISO currency, and active flag
- Returns the city regardless of its active state

**Use Cases:**
- Display a city's settings before editing
- Resolve a city referenced by a zone or trip

put.description:
Partially update a city's mutable attributes.

**Updatable Fields:**
- name, name_ar, timezone, currency, active
- Every field is optional; omitted fields are left unchanged (partial update)

**Restrictions:**
- Admin role required
- For multi-city activation with audit and ops events, prefer the dedicated activate/deactivate endpoints over flipping active here

400.description: Malformed JSON body (syntax error)

422.description: JSON body has a wrong-typed field

post.description:
Turn a city on for the multi-city expansion (Phase 11).

**Behavior:**
1. Loads the city; 404 if it does not exist
2. Rejects with 409 if the city is already active (this is a guard, not a no-op idempotent return)
3. Sets active = true and persists
4. Writes an audit row and fans out a beep.city.activated ops event

**Use Cases:**
- Bring a new city (e.g. Erbil, Basra, Mosul) online so its zones and trip logic begin matching

**Restrictions:**
- Admin role required
- The active flip propagates through existing city_id query filters; no schema change

409.description: City is already active

post.description:
Turn a city off (Phase 11).

**Behavior:**
1. Loads the city; 404 if it does not exist
2. Rejects with 409 if the city is already inactive (guard, not a no-op)
3. Sets active = false and persists
4. Writes an audit row and fans out a beep.city.deactivated ops event

**Use Cases:**
- Suspend a city so its zones and trip logic stop matching new demand

**Restrictions:**
- Admin role required
- This endpoint only flips the active flag; it does not cancel in-flight trips

409.description: City is already inactive

get.description:
Return the All-Customers directory with optional filters and pagination.

**Features:**
- Optional `phone` substring filter and `blocked` boolean filter.
- Each row carries name, phone, gender, total trips, cancellation count, blocked flag, and created time.
- Paginated: `page` is 1-based (clamped to >= 1); `per_page` defaults to 50 and is clamped to 1-200. The response echoes the effective `page` and `per_page`.

**Use Cases:**
- Browse and search the customer directory in the admin dashboard.

Return the detail overview for one customer.

**Returns:**
- Name, phone, gender, blocked flag and block reason, total trips, average rating received and given, cancellation count, and created time.
- Phone is shown in full (admin view, not proxied).

**Use Cases:**
- Render the customer profile drawer in the admin dashboard.

post.description:
Block a customer account with a recorded reason.

**Behavior:**
1. Trims and validates `reason` is at least 10 characters (400 otherwise).
2. Blocks the customer only if currently unblocked; publishes a `customer.blocked` audit event.
3. Only sets `blocked = TRUE`; it does NOT touch trips, so existing trips run to completion. Enforcement of the block (e.g. rejecting the customer's next OTP login) is handled in the auth domain, not here.
4. A missing customer returns 404; an already-blocked customer returns 409.
5. Returns `{ ok: true }`.

**Use Cases:**
- Suspend an abusive or fraudulent customer.

**Restrictions:**
- Super-admin only (403 otherwise).
- `reason` must be >= 10 characters after trimming.

400.description: Reason shorter than 10 characters

409.description: Customer already blocked

put.description:
Override the stored gender for a customer (used to gate women-only Abriyah).

**Updatable Fields:**
- `gender` only; must be `m`, `f`, or `unset` (400 otherwise).

**Behavior:**
1. Validates the gender value.
2. Applies the override; a missing customer returns 404.
3. Publishes a `customer.gender_overridden` audit event with the previous and new values.
4. Returns `{ ok: true }`.

**Restrictions:**
- Super-admin only (403 otherwise).
- Gender must be one of `m` | `f` | `unset`.

400.description: Invalid gender value

get.description:
Return the audit-log history for one customer as activity-feed rows.

**Features:**
- Reverse-chronological audit entries scoped to this customer (blocks, gender overrides, and other tracked events).
- Each row carries an event type, description, optional linked entity, and timestamp.
- Returns an empty list (not 404) for a customer with no audit history.

**Use Cases:**
- Show the customer's admin-action timeline in the profile drawer.

post.description:
Lift a block from a customer account.

**Behavior:**
1. Unblocks the customer only if currently blocked; publishes a `customer.unblocked` audit event.
2. A missing customer returns 404; a customer that is not blocked returns 409.
3. Returns `{ ok: true }`.

**Use Cases:**
- Restore access to a customer after review.

**Restrictions:**
- Super-admin only (403 otherwise).
- Takes no request body.

409.description: Customer is not blocked

get.description:
Return the most recent activity-feed rows, mapped from the audit log.

**Features:**
- Reverse-chronological highlights (newest first), each with an event type, human description, and optional linked entity.
- Only covers the last 24 hours: rows are filtered by `created_at > NOW() - INTERVAL '24 hours'`, so older activity never appears.
- `limit` is requested via the query param (defaults to 20) but is hard-clamped server-side to the range 1-20; the max returnable rows is 20 regardless of the requested value (e.g. `limit=50` still returns at most 20).

**Use Cases:**
- Populate the dashboard "recent activity" feed.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Max rows to return (defaults to 20); hard-capped at 20 even if a larger value is requested

get.description:
Return the 8 dashboard KPI cards plus yesterday deltas, computed in one query.

**Features:**
- Today-vs-now metrics: trips, completed, cancelled, active trips, open rooms, active captains, activations, and accrued activation fees (integer IQD).
- Includes `completed_yesterday` and `trips_yesterday` so the client can render deltas.
- "Today" is the Asia/Baghdad calendar day; counters roll at Baghdad midnight.

**Use Cases:**
- Render the top-of-dashboard KPI strip for any signed-in admin.

Return badge counts for the 4 Needs-Action tabs, excluding rows the calling admin dismissed.

**Features:**
- One count each for pending captains, flagged trips, expired rooms, and stuck items.
- `any_sla_breach` is true when at least one pending captain has waited more than 24h.
- The flagged-trips, expired-rooms, and stuck-items counts are per-admin: items this admin dismissed are not counted. The pending-captains count is global (it reflects the live approval queue) and is NOT per-admin, because pending captains cannot be dismissed.

**Use Cases:**
- Drive the sidebar Needs-Action badges without loading the full tab lists.

post.description:
Dismiss a Needs-Action item for the calling admin so it stops appearing in their counts and tabs.

**Behavior:**
1. Validates `item_type` is one of `flagged_trip`, `expired_room`, or `stuck_item` (400 otherwise).
2. Records a per-admin dismissal for the given `item_id`.
3. The item no longer shows in this admin's badge counts or tab lists; other admins are unaffected.
4. Returns `{ ok: true }`.

**Use Cases:**
- Clear an item the admin has already handled or judged not actionable.

**Restrictions:**
- Any admin may call this; the dismissal is scoped to the caller only.
- Pending captains cannot be dismissed (not an accepted `item_type`).

400.description: Unknown item_type

get.description:
Return the Needs-Action Tab 3 list: Abriyah rooms that expired within the last 24h.

**Features:**
- Each row carries zone name, room type, rider count at expiry, and open/expiry timestamps.
- Rows this admin has dismissed are excluded.

**Use Cases:**
- Populate the expired-rooms Needs-Action tab to investigate matching failures.

Return the Needs-Action Tab 2 list: trips flagged as long-running, late-cancelled, or low-rated.

**Features:**
- Each row carries the flag reason, trip type, captain/zone names, request time, status duration (minutes), and average stars.
- Rows this admin has dismissed are excluded.

**Use Cases:**
- Populate the flagged-trips Needs-Action tab for triage.

Return the Needs-Action Tab 1 list: captains awaiting approval.

**Features:**
- Each row carries name, phone, gender, registration time, wait `age_hours`, and whether documents are complete.
- `sla_breached` flags captains who have waited more than 24h.
- This tab is not per-admin dismissable; it always reflects the live approval queue.

**Use Cases:**
- Populate the captain-approval Needs-Action tab.

Return the Needs-Action Tab 4 list: stuck items (idle captains, no-ping trips, locked rooms).

**Features:**
- Each row carries an `item_type`, a related label, how long it has been stuck (minutes), and the last event time.
- Rows this admin has dismissed are excluded.

**Use Cases:**
- Populate the stuck-items Needs-Action tab to spot operational stalls.

List all saved UI preferences for the currently authenticated admin.

**Features:**
- Returns an array of preferences keyed by `(admin_user_id, pref_key)`, each with its `pref_value` and create/update timestamps.
- Implicitly scoped to the caller (taken from the bearer token subject); never returns another admin's preferences.
- No filtering, sorting, or pagination: returns every saved key for the admin in full.
- Empty array when the admin has saved no preferences yet.

**Use Cases:**
- Restoring saved view-mode toggles (e.g. table vs kanban) when the dashboard loads.

200.description: All preferences for the current admin

401.description: Missing, invalid, or revoked bearer token

403.description: Authenticated as a rider (admin role required)

put.summary: Upsert one of my UI preferences
put.description:
Create or update a single UI preference for the current admin, keyed by `pref_key`.

**Updatable Fields:**
- `pref_key` and `pref_value` from the request body. An existing key for this admin is overwritten; a new key is inserted.
- The preference is always scoped to the caller (token subject).

**Restrictions:**
- Admin-only (riders are rejected with 403).
- `pref_key` must be on the server allow-list (e.g. `operations.live_rooms.view`, `captains.pending.view`); an unknown key returns 400.
- `pref_value` must be one of the allowed values for that key (e.g. `table` / `kanban`, `table` / `inbox`); an invalid value returns 400.

400.description: Unknown preference key or value not allowed for that key

401.description: Missing, invalid, or revoked bearer token

403.description: Authenticated as a rider (admin role required)

delete.summary: Delete one of my UI preferences
delete.description:
Delete a single UI preference for the current admin by key.

**Behavior:**
- Removes the `(admin_user_id, pref_key)` row for the authenticated admin only.
- Idempotent: deleting a key that does not exist still returns 204 (no error).
- Returns no body.

**Restrictions:**
- Admin-only (riders are rejected with 403).
- Only ever affects the caller's own preferences.

204.description: Preference deleted (idempotent; also returned when the key was absent)

401.description: Missing, invalid, or revoked bearer token

403.description: Authenticated as a rider (admin role required)

get.summary: List live Abriyah rooms for ops
get.description:
Return live Abriyah room cards for the Operations Kanban.

**Features:**
- Filter by comma-separated `status` (defaults to open, locked, dispatched when omitted), `zone_id`, and `room_type`.
- Each card carries zone name, room type, status, current vs max riders, captain name, open time, and elapsed vs max wait (seconds).
- Not paginated; the default status set keeps the result to in-flight rooms.

**Use Cases:**
- Render the real-time Abriyah room Kanban, optionally scoped by zone or room type.

parameters.name: zone_id
parameters.description: Restrict to rooms in this zone

post.description:
Force-dispatch a locked Abriyah room (which already has a captain assigned).

**Behavior:**
1. Loads the room; a missing room returns 404.
2. Requires status `locked`; any other status returns 400.
3. Calls the room state machine's dispatch (guards status IN open,locked); a concurrent race surfaces as 400.
4. On success the room moves to `dispatched` and a `beep.room.dispatched` event (forced) is published.
5. Returns `{ ok: true }`.

**Use Cases:**
- Push a captain-assigned room into dispatch when auto-dispatch stalls.

**Restrictions:**
- Super-admin only (403 otherwise).
- Takes no request body.

400.description: Room is not in 'locked' state

post.summary: Force-expire an open or locked room
post.description:
Force-expire an Abriyah room that is still open or locked.

**Behavior:**
1. Loads the room; a missing room returns 404.
2. Requires status `open` or `locked`; any other status returns 400.
3. Calls the room state machine's expire; a concurrent race surfaces as 400.
4. On success the room moves to `expired` and a `beep.room.expired` event (forced, with reason) is published.
5. Returns `{ ok: true }`.

**Use Cases:**
- Tear down a room that will never match (e.g. no captains in zone).

**Restrictions:**
- Super-admin only (403 otherwise).
- The `reason` is recorded on the audit event.

400.description: Room is not in 'open' or 'locked' state

get.summary: List live trips for the ops map
get.description:
Return live trip pins for the Operations map.

**Features:**
- Filter by comma-separated `status` (defaults to requested, matched, accepted, in_progress when omitted), trip `type`, and `zone_id`.
- Each pin carries trip type, status, rider count, captain name/phone, captain lat/lng (WGS84, null until a captain pings), zone name, fare (integer IQD), and status duration in minutes.
- Not paginated; the default status set keeps the result to in-flight trips only.

**Use Cases:**
- Render the real-time ops map of active trips, optionally scoped to a zone.

parameters.name: zone_id
parameters.description: Restrict to trips in this zone

post.description:
Force-cancel a live trip as an admin, running the full cancellation cascade.

**Behavior:**
1. Runs the standard cancellation cascade with actor `admin` (admins may cancel accepted/in_progress trips that `system` cannot).
2. The `reason` is validated against the cancellation_reason enum inside the cascade; an invalid reason or illegal state returns 400.
3. On success the trip moves to `cancelled` and a `beep.trip.force_cancelled` event is published.
4. Returns `{ ok: true }`.

**Use Cases:**
- Operator intervention on a stuck or abusive live trip.

**Restrictions:**
- Super-admin only (403 otherwise).
- Goes through the trip state machine, so it cannot violate trip invariants.
- Unlike force-dispatch/force-expire (which return 404 for a missing room), an unknown trip id here returns 400, not 404: every cascade error, including trip-not-found, is mapped to a validation (400) failure.

400.description: Invalid reason, illegal trip state for cancellation, or unknown trip id (not-found is reported as 400 here, not 404)

get.description:
Admin queue of refund requests, optionally filtered by status, with limit/offset paging.

**Features:**
- Filter by lifecycle status; omit to list all.
- Paging: limit defaults to 50, clamped to 1..=200; offset defaults to 0.

**Use Cases:**
- Work the pending-refund review queue.
- Audit processed or rejected refunds over time.

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: Filter by status: pending | approved | rejected | processed

403.description: Caller holds a rider token (require_admin rejects only riders; captain and admin tokens pass)

post.description:
Open a refund request (status `pending`) against a succeeded transaction; an admin must later approve or reject it.

**Features:**
- Creating the request does NOT move money; approval is a separate step that reverses the charge.
- Emits a real-time refund-requested event.

**Behavior:**
1. Validate amount_iqd is positive.
2. Load the target transaction (404 if missing).
3. Reject if a refund already exists for that transaction (the original is by then `reversed`).
4. Reject if the transaction is not `succeeded`, or if amount_iqd exceeds the original amount.

**Restrictions:**
- amount_iqd must be > 0 and <= the original transaction amount.
- The transaction must be in `succeeded` state and not already refunded.
- Admin-only.

400.description: amount_iqd not positive, or transaction is not in a refundable (succeeded) state

403.description: Caller holds a rider token (require_admin rejects only riders; captain and admin tokens pass)

409.description: Transaction already refunded, or amount_iqd exceeds the original (over-refund)

get.description:
Admin lookup of a single refund request by id.

**Returns:**
- The full refund: transaction_id, amount_iqd (integer IQD), reason, status, refunded_by (the reviewing admin, null until reviewed), processed_at (null until processed), and any rejection_note.

**Use Cases:**
- Inspect a specific refund before approving or rejecting it.
- Confirm who processed a refund and when.

403.description: Caller holds a rider token (require_admin rejects only riders; captain and admin tokens pass)

post.description:
Approve and immediately process a pending refund: reverse the charge and credit the original wallet.

**Behavior:**
1. Load the refund; it must be in `pending` state.
2. Transition pending -> approved (guards against a concurrent double-approve).
3. If the original charge had a gateway reference, reverse it at the gateway.
4. Credit the originally-charged wallet by amount_iqd, write a `refund` ledger row, and mark the original transaction `reversed`.
5. Mark the refund `processed`, recording the approving admin, and emit a refund-processed event.

**Use Cases:**
- Finance approving a customer refund after review.

**Restrictions:**
- The refund must currently be `pending`; any other state returns 409.
- Admin-only.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Refund ID to approve and process

403.description: Caller holds a rider token (require_admin rejects only riders; captain and admin tokens pass)

404.description: Refund (or its underlying transaction) not found

409.description: Refund is not in the pending state

post.description:
Reject a pending refund request with an explanatory note; no money moves.

**Behavior:**
1. Load the refund; it must be in `pending` state.
2. Transition it to `rejected`, recording the reviewing admin and the note.
3. The original transaction is left untouched (no reversal, no wallet credit).
- If rejection_note is omitted in the body, the literal "rejected" is stored.

**Use Cases:**
- Decline a refund request that fails review, with a reason for the audit trail.

**Restrictions:**
- The refund must currently be `pending`; any other state returns 409.
- Admin-only.

403.description: Caller holds a rider token (require_admin rejects only riders; captain and admin tokens pass)

409.description: Refund is not in the pending state

get.description:
List Abriyah rooms for operational monitoring, with optional status and zone filters.

**Features:**
- Optional `status` filter ('open' | 'locked' | 'dispatched' | 'expired'); omit to list rooms of every status (including 'dispatched' and 'expired'), capped at 200.
- Optional `zone_id` filter to scope to a single zone.
- Returns rooms only (no member rows); use the room-detail endpoint for members.

**Use Cases:**
- The Admin Dashboard monitoring live Abriyah pooling activity across zones.

**Restrictions:**
- Admin JWT required.

get.summary: Get an Abriyah room with members (admin)
get.description:
Retrieve a single Abriyah room and all of its members for operational inspection.

**Returns:**
- The room (zone, type, status, rider counts, assigned captain, expiry and timestamps).
- All room members with per-rider fare (integer IQD), distance, and linked trip id.

**Use Cases:**
- The Admin Dashboard drilling into a specific room from the rooms list.

**Restrictions:**
- Admin JWT required.

404.description: No room exists with this id

get.description:
Admin view of scheduled trips across all riders, with optional status and rider filters plus pagination.

**Features:**
- `status` filters by lifecycle state: 'pending', 'promoted', 'cancelled', or 'expired'; omit for all.
- `rider_id` filters to a single rider.
- Pagination via `limit` (default 100, clamped to 1-500) and `offset` (default 0, floored at 0).

**Use Cases:**
- Admin Dashboard monitoring of upcoming and historical scheduled bookings.
- Investigate a specific rider's scheduled-trip activity.

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: Filter by status: pending|promoted|cancelled|expired (omit for all)

parameters.name: rider_id
parameters.description: Filter to a single rider's scheduled trips

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: Number of rows to skip; default 0, minimum 0

get.description:
Fan a single free-text query across captains, users, trips, zones, and rooms for the admin command palette.

**Features:**
- Five entity searches run concurrently, then merge in a fixed priority order (captain > user > trip > zone > room).
- Each result carries a `kind`, label, optional sublabel, and an Admin Dashboard deep-link `url_path`.
- The query is trimmed; an empty/whitespace query returns an empty result set without hitting the database.
- Merged results are truncated to a hard cap of 50.

**Use Cases:**
- Global quick-jump search from the Admin Dashboard command palette.

parameters.name: q
parameters.description: Free-text query; matched against captains, users, trips, zones, and rooms

401.description: Missing, invalid, or revoked bearer token

403.description: Authenticated as a rider (admin role required)

get.description:
Return every org-level singleton setting row.

**Features:**
- Returns all seeded keys (pricing, room, activation, general) in one call.
- Each row carries the current value, the last `updated_by` admin, and `updated_at`.
- Read-only keys (`general.timezone`, `general.currency`) are listed but rejected by the write endpoint.

**Use Cases:**
- Populate the admin Settings screen.
- Inspect current pricing/room/activation configuration before editing.

get.summary: Get a single setting by key
get.description:
Return one org setting row by its dotted key.

**Returns:**
- The current `value` (always a string; numeric/boolean keys are stringified), plus `updated_by` and `updated_at`.

**Use Cases:**
- Read a specific config value (e.g. `pricing.regular_per_km_iqd`) without fetching the whole list.

put.description:
Update one org setting after key, read-only, and per-key range validation.

**Updatable Fields:**
- `value` only; the key is taken from the path. Always sent as a string.

**Behavior:**
1. Verifies the key exists; unknown keys 404.
2. Rejects read-only keys (`general.timezone`, `general.currency`) with 400.
3. Range/format-checks the value per key (e.g. `pricing.*_per_km_iqd` 100-10000, `room.default_max_riders` 2-6, `general.locale` ar-IQ|en, phone keys E.164-ish).
4. Persists and publishes a `setting.updated` audit event with before/after.

**Restrictions:**
- Super-admin only (403 otherwise).
- Out-of-range, unparseable, or read-only values return 400.

400.description: Value out of allowed range or read-only key

get.description:
Admin console of the global transaction ledger with optional filters and limit/offset paging.

**Features:**
- Filter by wallet, status, type, and a created-at time window (RFC3339, UTC).
- Combine filters freely; omitted filters are ignored.
- Paging: limit defaults to 50, clamped to 1..=200; offset defaults to 0.

**Use Cases:**
- Reconcile payments and investigate failed or reversed charges across all users.
- Pull a date-bounded slice of the ledger for an audit.

parameters.name: wallet_id
parameters.description: Restrict to a single wallet id

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: Filter by status: pending | succeeded | failed | reversed

parameters.name: tx_type
parameters.description: Filter by type: trip_fare | daily_fee | topup | refund | cancellation_penalty

403.description: Caller holds a rider token (require_admin rejects only riders; captain and admin tokens pass)

get.description:
Admin lookup of a single ledger transaction by id.

**Returns:**
- The full transaction: linked wallet/trip/activation ids (any may be null), tx_type, amount_iqd (integer IQD), status, gateway_ref, failure_reason, and timestamps.

**Use Cases:**
- Drill into one charge during a support or finance investigation.
- Resolve a gateway_ref or failure_reason for a disputed payment.

403.description: Caller holds a rider token (require_admin rejects only riders; captain and admin tokens pass)

get.description:
Return all admin user accounts.

**Features:**
- Each row carries email, name, role, status, disabled flag, last-login time, and created time.
- Password hashes are never exposed.

**Use Cases:**
- Populate the admin-user management table.

post.description:
Create a new admin user in `invited` status and optionally email the invite.

**Features:**
- Email is trimmed and lowercased before storage.
- When `send_invite` is true (default) the invite email is enqueued to the `send-invite-email` queue; when false the account is created without sending (it can be sent later via resend-invite).
- Publishes an `admin.invited` audit event.

**Behavior:**
1. Validates email format (must contain `@` and `.`) -> 400.
2. Validates `role` is `super_admin`, `operator`, or `finance` -> 400.
3. Rejects a duplicate email with 409.
4. Inserts the row and returns it with 201.

**Restrictions:**
- Super-admin only (403 otherwise).
- Email must be unique and `role` must be a known role.

400.description: Invalid email format or unknown role

409.description: Email already exists

delete.description:
Permanently remove an admin user. Super-admin only.

**Behavior:**
- Rejects deleting your own account (the actor id from the JWT must differ from the target id) with 400.
- Loads the target admin; returns 404 if it does not exist.
- If the target is a `super_admin`, counts remaining super-admins and refuses to delete the LAST one (400) so the org is never locked out.
- Deletes the row, then publishes a `beep.admin.deleted` event on the `rt:admin:ops` channel for the audit trail.

**Restrictions:**
- Requires a valid bearer JWT with the `super_admin` role (enforced by `require_role` middleware).
- Irreversible: the admin row is hard-deleted, not soft-disabled (use the PATCH `disabled` flag to merely suspend).

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: UUID of the admin user to delete

400.description: Cannot remove your own account or the last super-admin

404.description: No admin user with that id

patch.summary: Update an admin user's profile or role
patch.description:
Partially update an existing admin user. Super-admin only.

**Updatable Fields:**
- `name`: display name.
- `role`: one of `super_admin`, `operator`, or `finance`.
- `disabled`: when true the account can no longer log in.
- Each field is optional; omitted fields are left unchanged via SQL `COALESCE` (true partial update).

**Behavior:**
- Validates `role` (when provided) against the allowed set BEFORE touching the database; an invalid value returns 400.
- Bumps `updated_at` to now and returns the full updated admin record.

**Restrictions:**
- Requires a valid bearer JWT with the `super_admin` role (enforced by `require_role` middleware).
- Returns 404 if no admin with the given id exists.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: UUID of the admin user to update

400.description: Invalid role (not super_admin, operator, or finance)

404.description: No admin user with that id

post.description:
Disable an admin account, blocking their sign-in.

**Behavior:**
1. Loads the target; a missing id returns 404.
2. Blocks disabling the last active (not-already-disabled) super-admin with 409.
3. Sets `disabled = true` AND revokes the target's outstanding tokens by bumping `tokens_valid_after = NOW()`, so any already-issued JWTs stop working immediately; publishes an `admin.disabled` audit event.
4. Returns `{ ok: true }`.

**Use Cases:**
- Revoke an admin's access without deleting the account.

**Restrictions:**
- Super-admin only (403 otherwise).
- Cannot disable the last active super-admin.

409.description: Cannot disable the last active super-admin

post.description:
Re-enable a previously disabled admin account.

**Behavior:**
1. Loads the target; a missing id returns 404.
2. Sets `disabled = false` and publishes an `admin.enabled` audit event.
3. Returns `{ ok: true }`.

**Use Cases:**
- Restore access to an admin after a suspension.

**Restrictions:**
- Super-admin only (403 otherwise).
- Takes no request body.

Re-queue the invite email for an admin still in `invited` status.

**Behavior:**
1. Loads the admin; a missing id returns 404.
2. Requires status `invited`; any other status (e.g. active) returns 409.
3. Re-enqueues the invite to the `send-invite-email` queue and publishes an `admin.invite_resent` audit event.
4. Returns `{ ok: true }`.

**Use Cases:**
- Resend an invite that expired or was never delivered.

**Restrictions:**
- Super-admin only (403 otherwise).
- Only valid while the target is in `invited` status.

409.description: Admin user is not in 'invited' status

put.description:
Change an admin's role, with last-super-admin and self-demotion guards plus token revocation on downgrade.

**Updatable Fields:**
- `role` (must be `super_admin`, `operator`, or `finance`) and `confirm_self_demotion`.

**Behavior:**
1. Validates the new role -> 400.
2. Loads the target; a missing id returns 404.
3. Same role as current is a no-op success.
4. Blocks demoting the only active super-admin with 409 (last-super-admin).
5. When the actor demotes themselves, requires `confirm_self_demotion = true`, else 400.
6. On any actual (non-no-op) role change, whether an upgrade or a downgrade, publishes a role-changed event. Only on a downgrade (role rank super_admin > operator > finance) does it additionally revoke the target's outstanding tokens by setting `tokens_valid_after = NOW()`.
7. Returns `{ ok: true }`.

**Restrictions:**
- Super-admin only (403 otherwise).
- Cannot strand the org without a super-admin; self-demotion needs explicit confirmation.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Admin user ID whose role to change

400.description: Invalid role or unconfirmed self-demotion

409.description: Cannot demote the last active super-admin

get.description:
Admin lookup of any rider or captain wallet by owner id and owner type.

**Returns:**
- The matching wallet: id, owner_id, owner_type, balance_iqd (integer IQD), and timestamps.
- Unlike the self-scoped wallet read, this does NOT auto-provision; an owner with no wallet yet returns 404.

**Use Cases:**
- Support and finance staff inspecting a user's balance during a dispute.
- Confirming a wallet exists before an admin credit.

parameters.name: owner_id
parameters.description: Rider or captain user ID that owns the wallet

parameters.name: owner_type
parameters.description: Wallet owner type to look up: "rider" or "captain"

403.description: Caller holds a rider token (require_admin rejects only riders; captain and admin tokens pass)

404.description: No wallet for that owner_id and owner_type

post.description:
Admin credit to a rider or captain wallet, e.g. a cash-desk top-up, without charging a real card.

**Features:**
- Routes through the standard top-up flow with no payment_method_id, so no saved card is charged.
- Auto-provisions the target wallet if it does not exist, writes a `topup` ledger row, and credits balance_iqd on success.

**Behavior:**
1. Validate amount_iqd is positive.
2. Record a pending `topup` transaction against the owner's wallet.
3. Settle the credit and emit a real-time top-up event.

**Restrictions:**
- amount_iqd must be > 0 (otherwise 400).
- owner_type is NOT validated here; it is passed straight into the wallet lookup and cast to the wallet_owner_type enum in SQL. A value other than "rider" or "captain" fails the enum cast as a database error and surfaces as 500, not a clean 400.
- Admin-only (require_admin gating).

parameters.name: owner_id
parameters.description: Rider or captain user ID whose wallet to credit

400.description: amount_iqd is not positive

403.description: Caller holds a rider token (require_admin rejects only riders; captain and admin tokens pass)

500.description: Credit could not be settled, or owner_type is not a valid wallet_owner_type enum value (enum-cast database error)

get.description:
Retrieve service zones with optional filtering.

**Features:**
- Filter by city_id to scope to a single city
- active query flag: when true, returns only active zones; when false or omitted, returns all zones including archived ones
- Each zone carries its polygon as WKT (lng/lat, SRID 4326) plus Abriyah pricing and room settings
- No pagination; returns the full matching set

**Use Cases:**
- Render the zone map and pricing table in the Admin Dashboard
- Audit archived zones for a city

parameters.name: city_id
parameters.description: Filter to zones in this city UUID

parameters.name: active
parameters.description: When true, return only active zones; when false or omitted, return all zones including archived

post.description:
Create a service zone from a WKT polygon and pricing settings.

**Features:**
- Omitted room and Abriyah pricing fields default from the settings singleton, then to hard defaults (room max riders 4, max wait 300s)
- regular_only zones have any per-km rate stripped to null
- On success, writes an audit row and publishes a beep.zone.created event

**Behavior:**
1. Validates zone_type is regular_only or abriyah_enabled (400 otherwise)
2. Verifies the referenced city exists (404 otherwise)
3. Runs a PostGIS validity check on the polygon (400 if invalid)
4. Resolves defaults, then enforces Abriyah completeness
5. Inserts the zone and returns the persisted record

**Restrictions:**
- Admin role required
- Polygon must be a closed shape, >= 4 points, no self-intersections; WKT is longitude-first (lng lat), SRID 4326
- abriyah_enabled zones require abriyah_per_km_iqd > 0 after defaults are applied (400 otherwise)
- Money fields are integer IQD

400.description: Invalid polygon, unknown zone_type, missing Abriyah per-km rate, or malformed JSON body (syntax error)

422.description: JSON body is missing a required field (e.g. polygon_wkt) or has a wrong-typed field

post.description:
Import many zones at once from beep.zone rows, committing the valid ones.

**Features:**
- Partial-commit: each row is validated and committed independently; one bad row never aborts the batch
- City is resolved by name (city_name), not UUID
- Each committed zone publishes a beep.zone.created event tagged via=import
- Always returns 200 with a report: committed count, failed count, and a per-row error list keyed by 1-based row index

**Behavior:**
- Per row, rejects (and records the message) when: zone_type is unknown, the named city is not found, an abriyah_enabled row lacks abriyah_per_km_iqd > 0, or the polygon fails the PostGIS validity check
- regular_only rows have any per-km rate dropped to null

**Restrictions:**
- Admin role required
- Polygons are WKT, longitude-first, SRID 4326; money fields are integer IQD

200.description: Import report with committed/failed counts and per-row errors

400.description: Malformed JSON body (syntax error)

422.description: JSON body is missing the required rows field or has a wrong-typed field

post.description:
Check whether a WKT polygon is well-formed without persisting anything.

**Features:**
- Runs the same PostGIS validity check used by zone create/update
- Side-effect free: no zone is created and no event is published
- Returns { "valid": true|false } rather than an error for an invalid shape

**Behavior:**
- valid is false when the polygon is not a closed shape, has fewer than 4 points, or self-intersects
- Input is WKT, longitude-first (lng lat), SRID 4326

**Use Cases:**
- Live-validate a drawn boundary in the Admin Dashboard before submitting create/update

**Restrictions:**
- Admin role required

400.description: Malformed JSON body (syntax error)

422.description: JSON body is missing the required polygon_wkt field or has a wrong-typed field

get.description:
Fetch a single zone record by its UUID.

**Returns:**
- Full zone record including the polygon as WKT (lng/lat, SRID 4326), zone_type, Abriyah per-km and base fare (integer IQD), women-only flag, and room settings
- Returns the zone regardless of its active/archived state
- Money fields are integer IQD with no decimals

**Use Cases:**
- Load a zone's full configuration before editing its boundary or pricing

put.description:
Partially update a zone's attributes and/or boundary polygon.

**Updatable Fields:**
- name, name_ar, polygon_wkt, zone_type, abriyah_per_km_iqd, abriyah_base_fare_iqd, allow_women_only, room_max_riders, room_max_wait_seconds, active
- Every field is optional; omitted fields keep their current value (partial update)
- On success, writes an audit row and publishes a beep.zone.updated event

**Restrictions:**
- Admin role required
- If zone_type is supplied it must be regular_only or abriyah_enabled (400 otherwise)
- If polygon_wkt is supplied it must pass the PostGIS validity check (400 otherwise); WKT is longitude-first, SRID 4326
- When the resulting zone_type is abriyah_enabled, the effective abriyah_per_km_iqd (new value or existing) must be > 0 (400 otherwise)
- Money fields are integer IQD

400.description: Invalid polygon, unknown zone_type, missing Abriyah per-km rate, or malformed JSON body (syntax error)

422.description: JSON body has a wrong-typed field

post.description:
Soft-archive a zone so it stops matching new demand.

**Behavior:**
1. Loads the zone; 404 if it does not exist
2. Counts active trips and open rooms still bound to the zone
3. If either count is non-zero, rejects with 409 and reports both counts in the error message
4. Otherwise marks the zone archived (active = false, archived_at and archived_by set) and publishes a beep.zone.archived event

**Use Cases:**
- Retire a zone whose boundary or pricing is being replaced

**Restrictions:**
- Admin role required
- Cannot archive while any active trip or open room references the zone (409); drain or force-cancel them first

409.description: Archive blocked: active trips or open rooms remain

put.description:
Apply a pricing- and room-settings-only change to an active zone.

**Updatable Fields:**
- abriyah_per_km_iqd, abriyah_base_fare_iqd, allow_women_only, room_max_riders, room_max_wait_seconds
- Every field is optional; omitted fields keep their current value (partial update)
- Boundary, name, and zone_type are NOT touched by this endpoint
- Money fields are integer IQD

**Behavior:**
1. Loads the zone; treats a missing OR inactive (archived) zone as 404
2. Captures a before snapshot, applies the change, captures an after snapshot
3. Counts in-flight trips locked to the old pricing and returns it as in_flight_trips_locked
4. Records a before/after audit entry and fans out a beep.zone.updated event for cache invalidation

**Restrictions:**
- Admin role required
- Pricing can only be changed on an active zone; an archived zone returns 404

200.description: Pricing updated; body includes the in-flight trip count locked to old pricing

400.description: Malformed JSON body (syntax error)

404.description: No active zone with this ID (missing or archived)

422.description: JSON body has a wrong-typed field

post.description:
Bring a previously archived zone back online.

**Behavior:**
1. Loads the zone; 404 if it does not exist
2. Rejects with 409 if the zone is already active (nothing to restore)
3. Otherwise marks the zone active again and publishes a beep.zone.updated event

**Use Cases:**
- Reverse an accidental archive or re-open a seasonally suspended zone

**Restrictions:**
- Admin role required
- Only an inactive (archived) zone can be restored; an already-active zone returns 409

409.description: Zone is already active

post.summary: Log in an admin with email and password
post.description:
Authenticate a dashboard admin by email and password and mint a role-bearing JWT.

**Behavior:**
- Looks up the admin by email; an unknown email and a wrong password return the SAME 401 (the response never reveals whether the email exists).
- Rejects the login with 403 when the account is disabled.
- Verifies the password against the stored argon2 hash.
- Stamps `last_login_at` to now on success.
- Issues a JWT whose claims carry the admin's id and role (`super_admin` | `operator` | `finance`).

**Returns:**
- `token`: signed JWT bearer credential for subsequent admin requests.
- `user_id`: the admin's UUID.

**Use Cases:**
- Admin Dashboard sign-in screen.

**Restrictions:**
- No bearer token required (this is the public login route).
- Email and password are both required in the body.

401.description: Unknown email or wrong password (indistinguishable by design)

post.summary: Verify captain OTP and issue a JWT
post.description:
Verify a login OTP for an already-registered, admin-approved captain and return a captain bearer token.

**Features:**
- Reuses the shared OTP delivery flow: codes are sent via `POST /api/auth/otp/send`, the same as riders.
- Issues a captain JWT whose `sub` is the captain's id; the returned `user_id` matches that captain id.

**Use Cases:**
- An already-registered, admin-approved captain completes phone login by submitting the code from `POST /api/auth/otp/send` to obtain a captain bearer token.
- The Captain App authenticates a driver before granting access to trip and activation features.

**Behavior:**
1. Normalize the phone, load the active OTP challenge, enforce the attempt cap, and verify the code against the stored Argon2id hash; the challenge is consumed on success.
2. Look up the captain by phone. Unlike the rider path, this NEVER creates an account.
3. Mint the token only if the captain's status is exactly `approved`.

**Restrictions:**
- A missing or expired OTP, or a wrong code, returns 401.
- More than 5 attempts against the same challenge, or an invalid phone, returns 400.
- No captain registered for the phone returns 404.
- A captain whose status is not `approved` (pending, rejected, or blocked) returns 403; approval is the identity gate, backed by the uploaded national-ID documents.

400.description: Invalid phone, or too many attempts against this OTP challenge

403.description: Captain exists but is not approved yet

404.description: No captain account is registered for this phone

post.summary: Send a login OTP to a phone
post.description:
Issue a one-time passcode for phone-based login and enqueue it for delivery.

**Features:**
- Used by both riders and captains; the same code works for either verify endpoint.
- Generates a numeric code (configurable length), Argon2id-hashes it, and stores an active challenge.
- Delivery is durable: the plaintext code is pushed onto the `send_otp` Redis queue and sent by a background worker, so a 200 means enqueued, not yet delivered.

**Use Cases:**
- A rider or captain starts phone-based login and needs a fresh code sent to their device.
- The user requested to resend the code after the previous one expired or never arrived.

**Behavior:**
- Normalizes the phone: strips a leading `+` and requires 10-15 digits.
- Rate limited to a configured number of issuances per rolling 10-minute window per phone.
- Each new send creates a fresh challenge with its own expiry.

**Restrictions:**
- Phone must be 10-15 ASCII digits (a leading `+` is allowed and stripped); otherwise 400.
- Exceeding the per-phone send rate limit returns 400.

200.description: OTP generated and enqueued for delivery

400.description: Invalid phone number, or the per-phone OTP rate limit was exceeded

post.summary: Verify rider OTP and issue a JWT
post.description:
Verify a login OTP for a rider, provision the rider account, and return a bearer token.

**Features:**
- Upserts the rider by phone: creates the account on first login, otherwise reuses it. An optional `name` is applied on the upsert.
- Issues a rider JWT whose `sub` is the rider's user id; the returned `user_id` matches `sub`.
- On a brand-new registration, publishes an admin-ops audit event (`beep.rider.registered`); event failures are swallowed and never block login.

**Use Cases:**
- A rider completes phone login by submitting the code from `POST /api/auth/otp/send` to obtain a bearer token.
- First-time riders register and authenticate in a single call, optionally setting their display `name`.

**Behavior:**
1. Normalize the phone and load the active OTP challenge; absent or expired challenges fail.
2. Enforce the per-challenge attempt cap, increment the attempt counter, then verify the code against the stored Argon2id hash.
3. On success, consume the challenge so the code cannot be reused, then upsert the rider and mint the token.

**Restrictions:**
- A missing or expired OTP, or a wrong code, returns 401.
- More than 5 attempts against the same challenge, or an invalid phone, returns 400.
- A blocked rider account is refused with 403.

200.description: OTP verified; rider upserted and JWT issued

400.description: Invalid phone, or too many attempts against this OTP challenge

get.description:
Resolve whether the authenticated captain has activated for the current Asia/Baghdad day.

**Returns:**
- `activated` -- true when an activation row exists for today, false otherwise.
- `activation` -- the full daily-activation record when activated (id, date, fee in integer IQD, status pending/paid/waived/failed, charge_error on a failed charge); null when not yet activated.

**Behavior:**
- The captain id is taken from the JWT subject; no path or query input.
- The day rolls over at Asia/Baghdad midnight, so a fresh day reads back `activated=false` until the captain taps Activate Today.

**Use Cases:**
- Captain App home screen deciding whether to show the Activate Today CTA or the active-for-today badge.
- Pre-check before letting the captain go online (the online toggle requires a same-day activation).

**Restrictions:**
- Requires a captain bearer token (the route is gated to role `captain`; a rider token is rejected with 403).
- The captain must exist and be `approved`; an unapproved captain is rejected with 403, an unknown captain with 404.

200.description: Gate status for today (activated flag plus the activation record when present)

403.description: Token is not a captain role, or the captain is not approved

post.summary: Tap Activate Today and charge the daily fee
post.description:
Activate the authenticated captain for the current Asia/Baghdad day and collect the daily activation fee from the captain wallet.

**Features:**
- Idempotent per day: a re-tap of an already-activated day returns the existing row without re-charging.
- The fee is read from the `activation.daily_fee_iqd` setting (integer IQD), falling back to a built-in default when the setting is unset.
- Emits a real-time `beep.captain.activated_today` event to the captain WS session and best-effort updates the reports rollup with the post-charge status.

**Behavior:**
1. The captain id is taken from the JWT subject; the request body has no fields in v1.
2. A pending activation row is created idempotently for today (Asia/Baghdad).
3. The captain wallet is charged the fee: on success the row flips pending -> paid (collected_at set); on insufficient funds it flips pending -> failed (charge_error set) and the call returns 402 so the CTA persists for top-up and retry.
4. The response is re-fetched after the charge, so the returned status reflects `paid`.

**Restrictions:**
- Requires a captain bearer token (the route is gated to role `captain`; a rider token is rejected with 403).
- The captain must exist and be `approved`; an unapproved captain is rejected with 403, an unknown captain with 404.
- A failed charge (insufficient wallet balance) returns 402 PaymentRequired; the captain tops up the wallet and taps again.

201.description: Activated for today; body is the activation record with status paid (or the existing row on an idempotent re-tap)

402.description: Insufficient wallet balance; the activation row is marked failed and the captain must top up and retry

403.description: Token is not a captain role, or the captain is not approved

get.description:
Read the authenticated captain's own last-known location row.

**Returns:**
- `captain_id`, `longitude`/`latitude` (WGS-84), `last_ping_at` (RFC3339), and the `online` flag.
- Reflects whatever the most recent ping or online toggle wrote; this is the captain's own record only, not another captain's.

**Use Cases:**
- The captain app restoring its map pin and online state after a restart.
- Confirming a ping was persisted.

403.description: Authenticated token is not a captain

404.description: No location row on file for this captain (no ping yet)

post.description:
Record the captain's current GPS position and fan it out in real time.

**Features:**
- Upserts the captain's last-known location row (WGS-84 longitude/latitude).
- A ping implies presence, so it sets the captain `online = true`.
- Fans the position out over Redis to the captain's own pin channel and the admin live map; if the captain is on an active trip, it also pushes to that trip's channel so the rider sees the car move.
- No audit row is written per ping (real-time fan-out only).

**Behavior:**
1. Validate coordinates (longitude in [-180, 180], latitude in [-90, 90]).
2. Upsert the location row. On a transient DB write failure the ping is buffered to the Redis `queue:ping_flush` list and the error is surfaced so the app retries.
3. Publish the new position to the captain, admin-ops, and (if applicable) active-trip channels.

**Restrictions:**
- Requires a captain JWT (captain-role middleware).
- Out-of-range coordinates are rejected with 400.

200.description: Ping stored; returns the captain's current location row

400.description: Longitude or latitude out of range

403.description: Authenticated token is not a captain

post.description:
Flush the GPS pings the captain app buffered while offline.

**Features:**
- Accepts an ordered batch of pings collected during a connectivity gap.
- Applies a last-known interpolation policy: only the final ping in the batch is stored.
- Storing that ping runs the same path as a live ping: it sets the captain online and fans the position out to the captain pin channel, the admin live map, and any active-trip channel.

**Behavior:**
1. Take the last entry in `pings` (an empty array is rejected with 400).
2. Validate its coordinates (longitude in [-180, 180], latitude in [-90, 90]).
3. Upsert and fan out exactly as a single live ping would.

**Restrictions:**
- Requires a captain JWT (captain-role middleware).
- An empty `pings` array or out-of-range coordinates are rejected with 400.

200.description: Last ping stored; returns the captain's current location row

400.description: Empty pings array or out-of-range coordinates

403.description: Authenticated token is not a captain

put.description:
Set the captain's online availability, enforcing the daily activation gate.

**Updatable Fields:**
- `online` (bool) -- the only field; sets whether the captain is available for dispatch.

**Behavior:**
1. When going online, the daily activation gate is checked: an Asia/Baghdad-dated `captain_daily_activations` row for today in `pending`, `paid`, or `waived` status must exist, otherwise 403.
2. Persist the new online flag on the captain's location row.
3. When going offline, publish a fade-pin message to the captain's own channel and the admin live map so subscribers drop the pin.

**Restrictions:**
- Requires a captain JWT (captain-role middleware).
- Going online without a same-day activation row is rejected with 403.

403.description: Authenticated token is not a captain, or daily activation is required to go online

get.summary: List pending offers for the captain
get.description:
List the pending dispatch offers currently visible to this captain.

**Features:**
- Returns a single list mixing two offer types: pending regular trips and open Abriyah rooms (`offer_type` distinguishes them).
- Each offer carries pickup/dropoff coordinates, fare in integer IQD, optional `zone_id`, optional `room_type` (`mixed` | `women_only`, null for regular trips), and `created_at`.
- Women-only rooms are pre-filtered out for non-female captains (gender is read from the captain record before matching).

**Use Cases:**
- The captain app polling for offers it may accept while online.
- Showing the nearby trip/room queue on the captain dashboard.

200.description: Offers visible to this captain (may be empty)

403.description: Authenticated token is not a captain

404.description: No captain record found for this token

get.summary: Get captain-side proxy numbers for a trip
get.description:
Return the masked phone-proxy session for a live trip, requested by the captain assigned to that trip.

**Returns:**
- The `ProxySession`: `rider_proxy_number` and `captain_proxy_number` are the masked numbers each party dials; the real phone numbers are never exposed.
- `provider`, optional `provider_session_id`, and `expires_at` (RFC3339; session TTL is 2 hours from allocation).

**Behavior:**
- Lazily allocates on first access: if no non-expired session exists for the trip, one is created via the number-proxy provider and persisted.
- Idempotent within the TTL window: a second call returns the same non-expired session.
- An expired session is re-allocated in place: the same row (same `id` and `created_at`) is updated with freshly masked numbers and a new `expires_at`, rather than inserting a new row.

**Use Cases:**
- The Captain App shows a call button after accepting a trip, without revealing the rider's real number.

**Restrictions:**
- Requires the `captain` role (bearer token).
- The caller must be the captain assigned to this trip, otherwise 403.
- The trip must be `accepted` or `in_progress` and have a captain assigned, otherwise 409.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Trip ID to fetch the proxy session for

403.description: Caller is not the captain on this trip, or lacks the captain role

404.description: Trip (or its rider record) not found

409.description: Trip is not accepted/in_progress, or no captain is assigned/resolvable yet

post.description:
Marks a pending multi-stop waypoint as reached. Only the captain currently assigned to the trip may call this.

**Behavior:**
1. Load the trip and verify the caller is the trip's assigned captain.
2. Locate the stop within the trip and require it to still be 'pending'.
3. Set the stop to 'reached' and stamp `reached_at`.
- The request body `reached_at` is optional; when omitted the repository stamps the reach time.

**Use Cases:**
- Captain confirms arrival at an intermediate waypoint during an active regular trip.

**Restrictions:**
- Caller must be the captain assigned to the trip (else 403).
- The trip must exist and the stop must belong to it (else 404).
- The stop must be 'pending'; an already-reached stop returns 409.

parameters.name: trip_id
parameters.description: Trip ID the stop belongs to

403.description: Caller is not a captain, or not the captain assigned to this trip

404.description: No trip with this id, or no such stop on the trip

409.description: Stop is not pending (already reached)

get.description:
Paginated list of captains for the Admin Dashboard, with optional filtering. Admin-only.

**Features:**
- Optional filters by `status`, `city_id`, and `gender`, combined with AND.
- Page-based pagination: `page` defaults to 1, `per_page` defaults to 20 and is clamped to 1-100.
- Each row carries a count of uploaded documents alongside summary captain fields.
- Response includes `total` (matching rows), `page`, and `per_page` for client-side paging.

**Use Cases:**
- Operator browsing of the captain roster filtered by city or approval status.
- Building the captain-management table in the dashboard.

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: Filter by captain status: pending | approved | rejected | blocked

parameters.name: city_id
parameters.description: Filter to a single city by its UUID

200.description: Paginated captain list with total count

400.description: Malformed query parameter (e.g. non-UUID city_id or non-integer page/per_page)

403.description: Authenticated as a rider (admin role required)

post.description:
Import many captains in one request, committing valid rows and reporting per-row failures. Admin-only. Partial-commit: this always returns 200, even when some rows fail.

**Features:**
- Each row is validated independently for gender, phone format, and uniqueness (phone and plate) both within the batch and against the database.
- Valid rows are inserted as `pending` captains and emit `beep.captain.registered` (via `bulk_import`).
- Response reports `accepted`, `rejected`, and an `errors` list with the 1-based row index and a human-readable reason for each failure.

**Behavior:**
1. Walk rows in order, tracking phones and plates already seen in this batch.
2. For each row: validate gender, normalize phone, enforce within-batch and database uniqueness, then insert.
3. Collect failures without aborting the rest of the import.

**Restrictions:**
- Gender must be 'm' or 'f'; phone must be 10-15 digits.
- Phone and car plate must be unique within the batch and across existing captains.
- Row-level errors do NOT change the HTTP status; inspect `errors` in the body.

200.description: Import summary with accepted/rejected counts and per-row errors

403.description: Authenticated as a rider (admin role required)

get.description:
Paginated queue of captains awaiting approval, oldest registration first. Admin-only.

**Features:**
- Returns only captains in the `pending` status (a pre-filtered convenience over the general list).
- Ordered oldest-first so the longest-waiting captain surfaces at the top of the queue.
- Page-based pagination: `page` defaults to 1, `per_page` defaults to 20 and is clamped to 1-100.
- Each row carries an uploaded-document count to gauge approval readiness.

**Use Cases:**
- Driving the operator review/approval worklist.
- Showing the backlog of captains needing a decision.

200.description: Paginated list of pending captains, oldest first

400.description: Malformed query parameter (e.g. non-integer page/per_page)

403.description: Authenticated as a rider (admin role required)

post.description:
Self-service captain registration from the Captain App. Public, no auth. Creates a captain in the `pending` status awaiting admin review.

**Features:**
- Normalizes the phone (strips a leading '+', requires 10-15 digits) before storing.
- Gender is validated to 'm' or 'f' and is locked after creation.
- Publishes `beep.captain.registered` and enqueues an admin review notification.

**Behavior:**
1. Validate gender ('m' | 'f') and normalize the phone.
2. Reject if the phone is already registered.
3. Reject if the car plate is already registered.
4. Insert the captain as `pending` and emit the registration event.

**Restrictions:**
- Phone and car plate must be unique across all captains.
- `city_id` must reference an existing city. This is enforced at INSERT time by the database FK, not a pre-insert existence check: any DB constraint violation on insert is coerced to a 404, so a missing city is the typical (but not the only possible) cause.
- No authentication required.

400.description: Invalid gender or malformed phone number

404.description: A DB constraint failed on insert (typically a non-existent referenced city)

409.description: Phone or car plate already registered

get.description:
Retrieve a single captain's full lifecycle record by ID. Requires any valid bearer token (admin or the captain themselves).

**Returns:**
- The complete captain record: identity, vehicle details, `city_id`, `status` (pending | approved | rejected | blocked), rejection/blocked reasons, approval metadata, `avg_rating`, trip and cancellation counts, and the optimistic-lock `version`.
- `national_id` and `car_color` are nullable and may be omitted.

**Use Cases:**
- The Captain App loading the signed-in captain's own profile and status.
- The Admin Dashboard opening a captain detail view.

post.description:
Approve a captain currently in the `pending` status, transitioning them to `approved`. Admin-only.

**Behavior:**
1. Load the captain; 404 if not found.
2. Pre-check the current status is `pending`; if not, reject as an invalid transition (400). The conditional UPDATE also re-guards on `pending`, so a concurrent status change between the pre-check and the UPDATE yields a 409 (optimistic-lock loss).
3. Require all five required document types on file via the document gate; otherwise reject as incomplete (400).
4. Mark approved (records the approving admin from the bearer token), publish `beep.captain.approved`, and enqueue a captain notification.

**Use Cases:**
- Operator clearing a captain from the pending queue once documents check out.

**Restrictions:**
- Only `pending` captains can be approved (idempotent re-approval is not allowed).
- All required documents (driver_license, car_registration, captain_selfie, national_id_front, national_id_back) must be present.

400.description: Documents incomplete, or captain is not in pending status (pre-check)

403.description: Authenticated as a rider (admin role required)

409.description: Captain left the pending status concurrently (optimistic-lock loss / illegal transition)

post.description:
Block a captain currently in the `approved` status, transitioning them to `blocked`. Super-admin only.

**Behavior:**
1. Load the captain; 404 if not found.
2. Pre-check the current status is `approved`; if not, reject as an invalid transition (400). The conditional UPDATE re-guards on `approved`, so a concurrent status change after the pre-check yields a 409 (optimistic-lock loss).
3. Mark blocked with the supplied reason and publish `beep.captain.blocked`.
4. Enqueue a force-cancel job for the captain's active trips (reason `captain_blocked`) and a captain notification (push sign-out cue).

**Use Cases:**
- Removing a misbehaving captain from service and cancelling their in-flight trips.

**Restrictions:**
- Requires the `super_admin` role.
- Only `approved` captains can be blocked.

400.description: Captain is not in approved status (pre-check)

409.description: Captain left the approved status concurrently (optimistic-lock loss / illegal transition)

get.description:
List all of a captain's uploaded documents. Requires any valid bearer token.

**Returns:**
- One entry per uploaded document with `doc_type`, `url`, and `uploaded_at`.
- When managed storage is configured, each `url` is a short-lived presigned view URL rather than the raw stored object key; a presign failure falls back to the raw stored value.
- An empty array when the captain has uploaded nothing.

**Use Cases:**
- The Admin Dashboard rendering a captain's documents during review.
- The Captain App showing which documents are on file.

200.description: The captain's documents (presigned view URLs when storage is managed)

post.summary: Upload or replace a captain document
post.description:
Persist (or replace) one of a captain's required documents. Requires any valid bearer token.

**Features:**
- Upserts by document type, so re-submitting a type replaces the prior reference.
- Accepts either the `object_key` from the presigned-upload flow (preferred) or a full `url` (bring-your-own).
- When managed storage is configured, the stored object key is returned back as a short-lived presigned view URL rather than the raw key.

**Behavior:**
1. Validate `doc_type` against the allowed set.
2. Verify the captain exists.
3. Choose the stored reference: non-empty `object_key` first, else non-empty `url`.
4. Upsert the document and return it with a resolvable `url`.

**Restrictions:**
- `doc_type` must be one of: driver_license, car_registration, captain_selfie, national_id_front, national_id_back.
- Exactly one of `object_key` or `url` (non-empty) must be provided, else the request is rejected.

200.description: Document stored; `url` is a presigned view URL when storage is managed

400.description: Invalid doc_type, or neither object_key nor url provided

get.description:
Report whether a captain has uploaded every document type required for approval. Requires any valid bearer token.

**Returns:**
- `complete`: true only when all required types are present.
- `uploaded`: the document types currently on file.
