- `missing`: the required types not yet uploaded.
- Required types are: driver_license, car_registration, captain_selfie, national_id_front, national_id_back.
- An unknown captain ID yields an empty `uploaded` list and all types in `missing` (no 404).

**Use Cases:**
- The Captain App showing the captain which documents are still outstanding.
- The dashboard gauging whether a captain is ready for approval.

200.description: Completeness summary (complete flag, uploaded and missing types)

post.summary: Mint a presigned document upload URL
post.description:
Mint a short-lived presigned PUT URL so the client can upload one document directly to storage, bypassing this API. Requires any valid bearer token.

**Features:**
- Returns `upload_url` (presigned PUT target), `object_key`, and `expires_in` (seconds until the URL expires).
- The client PUTs the raw image bytes to `upload_url` directly, then persists the document via `POST /api/captains/{id}/documents` with the returned `object_key`.

**Behavior:**
1. Validate `doc_type` against the allowed set.
2. Verify the captain exists.
3. Ask the storage backend to presign an upload target for this captain and document type.

**Restrictions:**
- `doc_type` must be one of: driver_license, car_registration, captain_selfie, national_id_front, national_id_back.
- The returned URL is short-lived; upload before `expires_in` elapses.

400.description: Invalid doc_type

500.description: Storage backend failed to presign the upload URL

get.summary: Get a presigned view URL for a document
get.description:
Mint a short-lived presigned GET URL to view one specific captain document. Admin-only.

**Returns:**
- `view_url` (presigned GET URL) and `expires_in` (300 seconds).
- Open `view_url` to render the stored image during admin review.

**Behavior:**
1. Validate `doc_type` against the allowed set.
2. Locate the captain's document of that type; 404 if absent.
3. Presign a GET URL for the stored object.

**Use Cases:**
- Operator inspecting a specific document (e.g. driver_license) before approving.

parameters.name: doc_type
parameters.description: Document type: driver_license | car_registration | captain_selfie | national_id_front | national_id_back

200.description: Presigned view URL valid for 300 seconds

400.description: Invalid doc_type

403.description: Authenticated as a rider (admin role required)

404.description: Captain has no document of this type

500.description: Storage backend failed to presign the view URL

get.description:
Summarise a captain's earnings over a rolling period, net of the daily activation fee.

**Returns:**
- `gross_iqd`: sum of completed-trip fares since the period start (integer IQD).
- `activation_fee_iqd`: a single flat activation fee (2000 IQD) subtracted when gross is positive, else 0.
- `net_iqd`: gross minus the activation fee.
- `trip_count` and the echoed `period`.
- Period windows are rolling from now: `today`/`day` = last 24h, `week` = last 7d, `month` = last 30d. The activation fee is exact only for `today`; over week/month it is illustrative (a single fee, not per-day) in v1.

**Use Cases:**
- Captain app earnings dashboard for today, this week, or this month.

parameters.name: period
parameters.description: Rolling window: today | week | month (defaults to today)

400.description: Period is not one of today, week, month

get.description:
List a captain's completed trips within a rolling period, for an itemised earnings breakdown.

**Returns:**
- `items`: each completed trip the captain finished in the window, with its fare and supporting detail (integer IQD).
- The echoed `captain_id` and `period`.
- Period windows are rolling from now: `today`/`day` = last 24h, `week` = last 7d, `month` = last 30d.

**Use Cases:**
- Captain app drilling from the earnings summary into the underlying trips.

parameters.name: period
parameters.description: Rolling window: today | week | month (defaults to today)

200.description: Per-trip earnings history for the period

400.description: Period is not one of today, week, month

post.description:
Reset a captain currently in the `rejected` status back to `pending` for another review. Super-admin only.

**Behavior:**
1. Load the captain; 404 if not found.
2. Pre-check the current status is `rejected`; if not, reject as an invalid transition (400). The conditional UPDATE re-guards on `rejected`, so a concurrent status change after the pre-check yields a 409 (optimistic-lock loss).
3. Transition back to `pending`. Audit-only: no public event is published.

**Use Cases:**
- Giving a previously rejected captain a second chance after they re-submit documents.

**Restrictions:**
- Requires the `super_admin` role.
- Only `rejected` captains can be reconsidered.

400.description: Captain is not in rejected status (pre-check)

409.description: Captain left the rejected status concurrently (optimistic-lock loss / illegal transition)

post.description:
Reject a captain currently in the `pending` status with a coded reason, transitioning them to `rejected`. Admin-only.

**Behavior:**
1. Validate `reason` against the allowed reason codes; otherwise reject (400).
2. Load the captain; 404 if not found.
3. Pre-check the current status is `pending`; if not, reject as an invalid transition (400). The conditional UPDATE re-guards on `pending`, so a concurrent status change after the pre-check yields a 409 (optimistic-lock loss).
4. Persist the reason and optional comment, publish `beep.captain.rejected`, and enqueue a captain notification (the comment, if any, is used as the notification body).

**Use Cases:**
- Operator declining a captain whose documents or vehicle do not pass review.

**Restrictions:**
- `reason` must be one of: documents_invalid, vehicle_unfit, identity_mismatch, existing_account, other.
- Only `pending` captains can be rejected.

400.description: Invalid reason code, or captain is not in pending status (pre-check)

403.description: Authenticated as a rider (admin role required)

409.description: Captain left the pending status concurrently (optimistic-lock loss / illegal transition)

post.description:
Restore a captain currently in the `blocked` status back to `approved`. Super-admin only.

**Behavior:**
1. Load the captain; 404 if not found.
2. Pre-check the current status is `blocked`; if not, reject as an invalid transition (400). The conditional UPDATE re-guards on `blocked`, so a concurrent status change after the pre-check yields a 409 (optimistic-lock loss).
3. Transition back to `approved`. Audit-only: no public event is published.

**Use Cases:**
- Reinstating a captain after a block is resolved.

**Restrictions:**
- Requires the `super_admin` role.
- Only `blocked` captains can be unblocked.

400.description: Captain is not in blocked status (pre-check)

409.description: Captain left the blocked status concurrently (optimistic-lock loss / illegal transition)

get.description:
Reports that the process is alive without touching any dependency.

**Returns:**
- `status`: always the literal "ok".
- `version`: the running `beeb` crate version (semver), useful for confirming the deployed build.

**Behavior:**
- Pure in-process check: does NOT query Postgres or Redis, so a slow or down dependency cannot make this fail.
- Always returns 200 while the process can handle requests.
- No authentication required.

**Use Cases:**
- Kubernetes / orchestrator liveness probe (restart on failure).
- Quick "is the API up" smoke test.
- For load-balancer routing decisions use `/api/health/ready` instead, which also checks dependencies.

Reports whether the instance can actually serve traffic by probing its dependencies.

**Returns:**
- `status`: "ready" when both checks pass, "not_ready" when either fails.
- `database`: `true` when a Postgres `SELECT 1` succeeded.
- `redis`: `true` when Redis answered a `PING`.

**Behavior:**
- Runs a Postgres `SELECT 1` and a Redis `PING` on each call; any error (including failure to acquire a Redis connection) marks that dependency as not ready.
- Returns 200 only when database AND redis are both reachable; returns 503 if either is down.
- The same JSON body is returned for both 200 and 503, so the per-dependency booleans can be inspected on failure.
- No authentication required.

**Use Cases:**
- Kubernetes readiness probe / load-balancer gating: route traffic away from this instance until it recovers.
- Diagnosing which dependency is degraded via the `database` and `redis` flags.

200.description: Ready to serve: both Postgres and Redis reachable

503.description: Not ready: Postgres or Redis unreachable

post.summary: Register or clear my FCM push token
post.description:
Store (or remove) the caller's FCM device token used for push notification delivery.

**Features:**
- Works for both riders and captains: the token is written to the row matching the JWT role (the `users` table for a rider, the `captains` table for a captain).
- Sending `{"fcm_token": null}` (or omitting the field) clears the stored token -- call this on logout to stop notifications.

**Behavior:**
1. Resolve the account from the token subject and role.
2. For role `rider`, set the token on the existing rider record; for role `captain`, set it on the existing captain record (a plain UPDATE -- it never inserts a new row, and returns 404 when no row matches).
3. Return 204 No Content on success (no response body).

**Restrictions:**
- Only the `rider` and `captain` roles are supported; any other role is rejected with 403.
- The token is always set for the authenticated caller; there is no way to set another account's token.

204.description: Token stored or cleared; no response body

403.description: Token role is neither rider nor captain

404.description: No account exists for the token subject

get.description:
List every saved payment method (cards and wallet) belonging to the authenticated rider or captain.

**Features:**
- Scoped to the caller via the JWT subject and role; no filters or pagination.
- Each entry exposes method_type, masked last 4 digits (cards only), and the default flag. The raw card number and gateway token are never returned.

- Render the saved-cards picker before a wallet top-up.

post.description:

**Features:**
- For a card, the raw PAN is read only to derive the last 4 digits and a gateway token; only the masked last 4 and the token are stored, and the token is never returned.
- When set_as_default is true, the new method is promoted to default and the returned row reflects is_default = true.

**Behavior:**
1. If method_type is "card", require card_number, derive last 4, and tokenize via the gateway.
2. Persist the method scoped to the caller.
3. Optionally set it as the default.

**Restrictions:**
- method_type "card" requires card_number of at least 4 characters.
- Caller must be a rider or captain; admin roles cannot save personal methods.

Permanently remove one of the caller's saved payment methods.

**Restrictions:**
- The method id must belong to the caller; a non-matching id returns 404 and nothing is deleted.

**Returns:**
- The caller's wallet: id, owner_id, owner_type ("rider" or "captain"), balance_iqd (integer IQD, no decimals), and timestamps.

post.description:

**Features:**
- Auto-provisions the wallet if it does not yet exist.
- Writes a `topup` ledger transaction first (status `pending`), then attempts the gateway charge.

**Behavior:**
1. Validate amount_iqd is positive.
2. Resolve the saved method's gateway token (the method must belong to the caller).
3. Charge the gateway; on success credit the wallet and emit a real-time top-up event, on failure mark the transaction `failed`.

**Restrictions:**
- amount_iqd must be > 0.
- payment_method_id (when given) must be owned by the caller, otherwise the method is treated as not found.

Returns every scheduled trip booked by the authenticated rider, across all statuses.

**Features:**
- Scoped to the caller's rider id from the JWT; no filters or pagination.
- Includes pending, promoted, cancelled, and expired trips.
- Each row carries pickup/dropoff coordinates, the scheduled UTC time, status, and the promoted live trip id once promoted.

- Render the rider's upcoming and past scheduled-trip list in the Customer App.
- Confirm a booking exists and inspect whether it has been promoted to a live trip yet.

post.description:
Creates a future scheduled trip intent for the authenticated rider. The trip is stored as 'pending' and later promoted to a live REQUESTED trip by the scheduler worker when it comes due.

**Features:**
- Sets `rider_id` from the JWT and starts the trip in 'pending' status.
- Publishes a `beep.scheduled_trip.created` realtime event on the rider channel.
- Promotion to a live trip happens later via the 60-second scheduler tick, not on this call.

**Behavior:**
1. Validate `scheduled_for` is at least 30 minutes and at most 7 days from now.
2. Validate `trip_type` is exactly 'regular' (Abriyah scheduling is not supported in v1).
3. Persist the pending scheduled trip and emit the created event.

**Restrictions:**
- `scheduled_for` is RFC3339/ISO 8601 UTC; under 30 minutes ahead returns 400, over 7 days ahead returns 400.
- `trip_type` must be 'regular'; any other value returns 400.
- Coordinates are WGS84 lat/lng.

404.description: Persistence failed: the scheduled trip could not be written (edge/internal path)

- Coordinates are WGS84 lat/lng; timestamps are RFC3339.

**Use Cases:**
- Show the detail view for a single scheduled booking in the Customer App.
- Poll a booking to detect promotion (a non-null `promoted_trip_id`).

403.description: Caller is not a rider, or does not own this scheduled trip

put.description:
Edits a still-pending scheduled trip. Only the owning rider may update, and only while the trip is 'pending' (not yet promoted, cancelled, or expired).

**Updatable Fields:**
- `scheduled_for`, `pickup_lat`, `pickup_lng`, `pickup_address`, `dropoff_lat`, `dropoff_lng`, `dropoff_address`.
- Partial update: omitted fields are left unchanged.
- `trip_type` and status are not editable here.

**Restrictions:**
- Caller must own the trip (else 403) and the trip must exist (else 404).
- The trip must be 'pending'; if it has been promoted, cancelled, or expired the call returns 409.
- If `scheduled_for` is supplied it is re-validated to the 30-minutes-to-7-days window; outside that returns 400.
- Coordinates are WGS84 lat/lng; `scheduled_for` is RFC3339 UTC.

1. Load the scheduled trip and verify the caller owns it.
2. Require the trip to still be 'pending'; otherwise reject as already finalised.
3. Set status to 'cancelled' and record the cancellation reason.
- The request body `reason` is optional; when omitted the reason defaults to 'rider_cancelled'.

**Use Cases:**
- Rider abandons a future booking before the scheduler promotes it.

**Restrictions:**
- Caller must own the trip (else 403) and the trip must exist (else 404).
- Only 'pending' trips can be cancelled; an already promoted, cancelled, or expired trip returns 409.

Return a rider-safe summary of the captain assigned to the rider's own trip: name, vehicle (make/model/color/plate), average rating, and lifetime trip count.

**Access Control:**
- The trip must belong to the authenticated rider (`rider_id` = token subject); any other rider gets 403.
- Captain tokens are rejected with 403 (this is a rider-only view).

**Privacy:**
- No phone number or other captain PII is exposed -- only what the rider needs to identify the car pulling up.

**Behavior:**
1. Load the trip; 404 if it does not exist.
2. Verify the caller owns it; 403 otherwise.
3. 404 if no captain is assigned yet (trip still `requested`).
4. Return the captain summary.

get.summary: Get rider-side proxy numbers for a trip
get.description:
Return the masked phone-proxy session for a live trip, requested by the rider on that trip.

**Returns:**
- The `ProxySession`: `rider_proxy_number` and `captain_proxy_number` are the masked numbers each party dials; the real phone numbers are never exposed.
- `provider`, optional `provider_session_id`, and `expires_at` (RFC3339; session TTL is 2 hours from allocation).

**Behavior:**
- Lazily allocates on first access: if no non-expired session exists for the trip, one is created via the number-proxy provider and persisted.
- Idempotent within the TTL window: a second call returns the same non-expired session.
- An expired session is re-allocated in place: the same row (same `id` and `created_at`) is updated with freshly masked numbers and a new `expires_at`, rather than inserting a new row.

**Use Cases:**
- The Customer App shows a call button once a captain has accepted, without revealing the captain's real number.

**Restrictions:**
- Requires the `rider` role (bearer token).
- The caller must be the rider on this trip, otherwise 403.
- The trip must be `accepted` or `in_progress` and have a captain assigned, otherwise 409.

- `photo_url` -- avatar URL.

Partial update: every field is optional and only the fields present in the body are written; omitted fields are left unchanged. The full updated record is returned.

post.summary: Mint a presigned avatar upload URL
post.description:
Mint a short-lived presigned PUT URL so the rider can upload an avatar photo directly to storage, bypassing this API. Mirrors the captain-document upload flow.

**Returns:**
- `upload_url` -- presigned PUT target; PUT the raw image bytes here directly.
- `object_key` -- the deterministic key to persist via `PATCH /api/riders/me` as `photo_url`.
- `expires_in` -- seconds until `upload_url` expires.

**Flow:**
1. Call this endpoint to get `upload_url` + `object_key`.
2. PUT the image bytes to `upload_url` before it expires.
3. `PATCH /api/riders/me` with `{ "photo_url": "<object_key>" }`.
4. `GET /api/riders/me` returns `photo_url` as a short-lived presigned view URL.

**Notes:**
- The object key is per-rider, so a re-upload overwrites the previous avatar in place.
- With unmanaged (mock) storage the URLs are deterministic placeholders.

500.description: Storage backend failed to presign the upload URL

get.description:
List trips, optionally filtered by rider, captain, or status, with page-based pagination.

**Features:**
- Filters (all optional, combinable): `rider_id`, `captain_id`, `status`.
- Pagination: `page` defaults to 1 (min 1); `per_page` defaults to 20 and is clamped to 1..=100.
- Response carries `items`, plus the total matching count and the echoed `page` / `per_page`.

**Use Cases:**
- A rider listing their own trip history (`rider_id` = their id).
- A captain listing assigned trips (`captain_id` = their id).
- Admin browsing all trips in a given status.

parameters.name: rider_id
parameters.description: Filter to trips for this rider id

parameters.name: captain_id
parameters.description: Filter to trips assigned to this captain id

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: Filter by lifecycle status (e.g. requested, accepted, in_progress, completed, cancelled)

parameters.name: per_page
parameters.description: Items per page; defaults to 20, clamped to 1..=100

post.description:
Create a regular point-to-point trip request as the authenticated rider.

**Features:**
- Fare is computed server-side from a haversine distance between pickup and dropoff (WGS84 lat/lng) at the effective per-km rate.
- The effective per-km rate is the supplied zone's Abriyah rate when present and positive, otherwise the city default rate.
- Fare and base fare are returned in integer IQD (no decimals).
- On creation the trip is fire-and-forget dispatched: a background broadcast publishes `beep.trip.requested` to the nearest captains.

**Behavior:**
1. Reject if the rider already has an active (non-terminal) trip.
2. Compute distance, per-km rate, and total fare; create the trip in status `requested`.
3. Spawn the dispatch broadcast (failures are logged, not surfaced to the caller).

**Restrictions:**
- Requires a valid bearer token; `claims.sub` is used as the rider id.
- One active trip per rider; a second concurrent request is rejected with 409.

201.description: Trip created in `requested` and dispatch broadcast started

409.description: Rider already has an active trip

get.description:
Compute a pre-request fare estimate for a pickup/dropoff pair. Public: no authentication required.

**Returns:**
- `fare_iqd`: the total estimated fare in integer IQD (no decimals).
- `distance_km`: the haversine distance between pickup and dropoff (WGS84 lat/lng) in km.
- `base_fare_iqd`: the base fare component in integer IQD.
- `currency`: always `IQD`.
- The effective per-km rate used is the supplied zone's Abriyah rate when present and positive, otherwise the city default rate. `trip_type` is accepted for client convenience but does not change the computation.

**Use Cases:**
- Rider app showing an estimated price before the rider commits to a request.
- Abriyah pricing preview by passing a `zone_id`.

400.description: Malformed or missing query parameters

get.description:
Retrieve a single trip's full lifecycle record by id.

**Returns:**
- The complete `Trip` row: type, status, fare and base fare (integer IQD), distance, pickup/dropoff coordinates, captain id (null until accepted), per-rider fare/distance breakdown, and lifecycle timestamps.
- No participant filtering is applied: any authenticated rider, captain, or admin may read any trip by id.

**Use Cases:**
- Rider or captain polling the current state of their trip.
- Admin or support inspecting a specific trip.

post.description:
Captain claims a trip, moving it through the state machine to `accepted`.

**Behavior:**
1. Require a captain token; the captain id is taken from the JWT `sub`.
2. Validate the transition to `accepted` for the actor `captain` (`requested -> accepted` for regular, `matched -> accepted` for Abriyah).
3. Reject if this captain already has an active trip.
4. Apply the optimistic-lock accept (`accept` for `requested`, `accept_matched` for `matched`); any other current status is a conflict.
5. On success, publish `beep.trip.accepted` and enqueue a `trip_accepted` push to the rider.

**Use Cases:**
- Captain app tapping Accept on a dispatched trip offer.

**Restrictions:**
- Captain role only; a non-captain token is rejected with 403.
- One active trip per captain; a second accept is rejected with 409.
- Invalid source state yields 400 (illegal transition) or 409 (lost optimistic lock / stale status).

400.description: Invalid state transition to accepted

409.description: Captain already has an active trip, or the trip is no longer acceptable

post.description:
Signal that the captain has reached the pickup point. This is a cue event only and does not change the trip status.

**Behavior:**
1. Require a captain token; the captain id is taken from the JWT `sub`.
2. Require the trip to be in `accepted`; any other status yields 400.
3. Require this captain to be the trip's assigned captain; otherwise 403.
4. Publish `beep.trip.arrived` and enqueue a `captain_arriving` push to the rider.
5. Return the unchanged trip (status stays `accepted`).

**Use Cases:**
- Captain app tapping I Have Arrived to notify the rider.

**Restrictions:**
- Captain role only; a non-captain token is rejected with 403.
- Only the trip's own captain may mark arrival.
- Trip must be in `accepted` state.

400.description: Trip is not in the accepted state

403.description: Caller is not a captain, or not the trip's assigned captain

post.description:
Cancel a trip on behalf of the caller. The cancelling actor is derived from the JWT role: `rider`, `captain`, or `admin` (any admin role).

**Behavior:**
1. Validate the cancellation `reason` against the allowed enum (changed_mind, wait_too_long, wrong_pickup, captain_late, safety, system_timeout, captain_blocked, other); an unknown reason yields 400.
2. Pre-validate the transition to `cancelled` for the derived actor, then apply the optimistic-lock cancel.
3. Fan out side effects: notify the opposite party (rider-cancel pushes the assigned captain if any; captain/admin-cancel pushes the rider), and a captain bailing on an `accepted` trip triggers re-dispatch.
4. Best-effort cancellation penalty: a rider who cancels after the captain had already committed (`accepted` or `in_progress`) is charged the admin-tunable `trip.cancellation_penalty_iqd`; free while still `requested`/`matched`. A charge failure is logged, never surfaced.
5. Best-effort reports rollup of the cancelled trip and reason.
6. Publish `beep.trip.cancelled`.

**Use Cases:**
- Rider abandoning a request, captain dropping an accepted trip, or admin/support force-cancelling.

**Restrictions:**
- Requires a valid bearer token.
- The reason must be one of the allowed values; an invalid reason or illegal transition yields 400.
- Cancellation not allowed in the current state (or a lost optimistic lock) yields 409.

400.description: Invalid cancellation reason or illegal state transition

409.description: Cancellation not allowed in the current state, or lost optimistic lock

post.description:
End the ride, transitioning the trip `in_progress -> completed` and writing the per-rider fare/distance breakdown.

**Behavior:**
1. Validate the transition to `completed` for the actor `captain`.
2. Write a single-rider breakdown (this trip's fare in integer IQD and distance in km keyed by the rider id) and complete with the trip's current version.
3. Publish `beep.trip.completed` and enqueue a `trip_completed` push to the rider.
4. Best-effort reports rollup (trip volume, revenue-by-zone, leaderboard) -- failures are logged, never surfaced.
5. Best-effort wallet charge of the fare from the rider's wallet (v1 in-wallet MockGateway). An insufficient-balance debit is recorded as a `failed` ledger row and logged; it never blocks completion.

**Use Cases:**
- Captain app tapping Complete at the dropoff.

**Restrictions:**
- Requires a valid bearer token; the completing actor id is taken from the JWT `sub`.
- Trip must be in `in_progress`; an illegal transition yields 400.
- A lost optimistic lock (concurrent update) yields 409.

200.description: Trip completed; fare charge attempted best-effort

400.description: Invalid state transition to completed

409.description: Concurrent update conflict (lost optimistic lock)

get.summary: List all ratings for a trip
get.description:
Retrieve every rating attached to a trip (rider->captain and/or captain->rider).

**Returns:**
- `items`: the ratings recorded for this trip, each with stars (1-5), optional comment, the rater seat, and timestamps. An empty list is returned when the trip has no ratings (this is a 200, not a 404).

**Use Cases:**
- Showing both parties' ratings on a completed trip.
- Admin reviewing feedback for a specific trip.

post.summary: Submit a rating for a trip
post.description:
Submit a 1-5 star rating after a trip completes. Direction is derived from the caller's role: a rider rates the captain, a captain rates the rider.

**Features:**
- Rating direction is set automatically from the caller's JWT role (rider->captain or captain->rider); no direction field is sent.
- One rating per rater per trip is enforced; the rater seat is recorded from the token.
- On success a `beep.trip.rated` event is published.

**Behavior:**
1. Reject if `stars` is outside 1..=5.
2. Require the trip to exist and be `completed`; otherwise 404 or 400.
3. Reject if this rater already rated this trip (one rating per rater per trip).
4. Require the submitter to be the trip's rider (rider->captain) or captain (captain->rider); otherwise 403.
5. Persist the rating and publish `beep.trip.rated`.

**Use Cases:**
- Rider rating the captain on the post-trip screen.
- Captain rating the rider after dropoff.

**Restrictions:**
- Requires a valid bearer token; the role decides the rating direction.
- Trip must be completed; stars must be 1-5; only a trip participant may rate; one rating per rater.

400.description: Stars out of range 1-5, or trip is not completed

403.description: Caller is not a participant in this trip

409.description: Caller has already rated this trip

put.summary: Edit a rating within the 7-day window
put.description:
Update a previously submitted rating, allowed only within the 7-day edit window and only by the original rater.

**Updatable Fields:**
- `stars` and/or `comment`. When `stars` is supplied it must be 1-5; partial updates apply only the supplied fields.

**Behavior:**
1. Reject if a supplied `stars` is outside 1..=5.
2. Require the rating to exist; otherwise 404.
3. Require the caller (derived from the JWT role) to be the original rater; otherwise 403.
4. Reject if the rating is already locked (7-day window elapsed); the repository also re-checks `locked_at IS NULL` as a race-safe backstop.

**Restrictions:**
- Requires a valid bearer token; only the original rater may edit.
- Edits are blocked once the 7-day window has expired (400).
- Stars, if present, must be 1-5.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Trip id (UUID); path only, not used to locate the rating

400.description: Edit window expired, or stars out of range 1-5

403.description: Caller is not the original rater

post.description:
Begin the ride, transitioning the trip `accepted -> in_progress`.

**Behavior:**
1. Require a captain token; the captain id is taken from the JWT `sub`.
2. Validate the transition to `in_progress` for the actor `captain`.
3. Apply the optimistic-lock start using the trip's current version.
4. Publish `beep.trip.started`.

**Use Cases:**
- Captain app tapping Start once the rider is on board.

**Restrictions:**
- Captain role only; a non-captain token is rejected with 403.
- Trip must be in `accepted`; an illegal transition yields 400.
- A lost optimistic lock (concurrent update) yields 409.

400.description: Invalid state transition to in_progress

409.description: Concurrent update conflict (lost optimistic lock)

get.description:
Retrieve all active service zones for the Customer and Captain apps.

**Features:**
- No authentication required
- Returns only active zones; archived zones are never exposed
- Not filtered by city; returns active zones across all cities
- Each zone carries its polygon as WKT (lng/lat, SRID 4326) plus Abriyah pricing and room settings (integer IQD)

**Use Cases:**
- Draw serviceable areas on the rider map
- Let the Captain App know which zones are live

Fetch a single active service zone by UUID for the Customer and Captain apps.

**Returns:**
- The zone's polygon as WKT (lng/lat, SRID 4326), Abriyah per-km and base fare (integer IQD), women-only flag, and room settings
- No authentication required
- An archived (inactive) zone is treated as not found and returns 404, so archived zones are never exposed publicly

**Use Cases:**
- Look up the pricing and settings of a zone the rider tapped on the map

CaptainListResponse.description: Paginated page of captain list rows.
